channel.title: SettleWire
channel.description: Prediction markets news: regulation, volume, deals and data.

item.title: South Korea regulator to hear Polymarket defense before gambling ruling
item.description: South Korea's Broadcasting, Media and Communications Review Committee announced on Monday, July 6, 2026, it would let Polymarket submit a formal defense before deciding whether to issue a corrective order over illegal gambling concerns.

item.title: Polymarket Trader Bleeds $11M on World Cup 2026 Contracts
item.description: A single Polymarket trader lost more than $11 million wagering on 2026 FIFA World Cup outcome markets over just ten days.

item.title: Kalshi Hits $31B June Volume as World Cup Drives Record Trade
item.description: Kalshi set a new monthly trading record of $31 billion in June, fueled by World Cup event contracts even as multiple U.S. states moved to criminalize its activity.

item.title: State-by-State Legal Map: Where Sports Prediction Markets Stand
item.description: A new CBS Sports breakdown examines the patchwork of state-level legality governing sports prediction market platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket across all 50 U.S. states.

item.title: FIFA clears Balogun for Belgium, Polymarket trading surges
item.description: FIFA granted Folarin Balogun a rare Article 27 eligibility switch to represent Belgium, triggering a sharp spike in trading volume on Polymarket's nationality markets in early 2025.

item.title: Minnesota's Prediction Market Ban Takes Effect August 1
item.description: Minnesota becomes the first U.S. state to ban prediction markets when its new law takes effect August 1, setting a precedent that could ripple nationally.

item.title: Wildfire Prediction Markets Blur Line Between Hedging and Speculation
item.description: Prediction markets are now offering contracts tied to wildfire outcomes, raising questions about whether disaster betting serves a legitimate hedging function or exploits crisis.

item.title: Kalshi June Volume Hits $9.4B Record as World Cup Drives Surge
item.description: Kalshi posted its highest-ever monthly trading volume in June at nearly $9.4 billion, with the expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup serving as the primary catalyst.

item.title: World Cup Betting Pushes Prediction Market Volumes to All-Time Highs
item.description: The FIFA World Cup has triggered record-breaking trading volumes across major prediction market platforms, marking a new milestone for the industry.

item.title: Prediction Markets Boom Raises Questions About Regulatory Durability
item.description: A surge in prediction market activity has prompted fresh scrutiny over whether the industry's rapid growth can survive tightening regulatory pressure.

item.title: Michigan's Legal Fight Against Kalshi Reaches New Turning Point
item.description: Michigan's ongoing regulatory dispute with prediction-market operator Kalshi has entered a new phase, continuing a clash over whether the platform's event contracts fall under state sports-betting law.

item.title: EU Classifies Event Contracts as Binary Options, Triggering Strict Rules
item.description: European regulators have formally classified event contracts as binary options under EU law, subjecting prediction-market platforms to the bloc's strict binary-options regulatory framework.

item.title: Michigan Daily: Prediction Markets Deserve Recognition as Public Good
item.description: The Michigan Daily published an opinion piece arguing that prediction markets generate genuine social value beyond speculation.

item.title: Spotify Nukes 500K Streams Tied to Prediction Market Manipulation
item.description: Spotify removed roughly 500,000 streams from a popular track after suspecting users gamed the platform to influence Kalshi and Polymarket event contracts.

item.title: Unusual Polymarket Bets Spark Speculation on Putin Political Future
item.description: Large, lopsided wagers on a Polymarket contract about Vladimir Putin's hold on power have raised eyebrows among traders and political observers alike.

item.title: ESMA: Most EU Prediction Market Event Contracts Already Banned for Retail
item.description: Europe's top securities regulator has warned that a large share of prediction market event contracts are already prohibited for retail investors under existing EU law.

item.title: Spotify Demands Kalshi and Polymarket Drop Music Betting Markets
item.description: Spotify has formally asked prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket to remove its branding after manipulators gamed stream counts to influence music-related contract settlements.

item.title: Prediction Market Daily Volume Jumps 80% in June, Sports Dominates
item.description: Prediction market daily trading volume surged 80% in June, with sports-related contracts driving nearly nine-tenths of all activity, according to a new Jefferies analysis.

item.title: U.S. Users Moved $571M on Polymarket Despite American Ban
item.description: American traders collectively wagered $571 million on Polymarket's political event contracts even though the platform is prohibited from serving U.S. customers.

item.title: Judge Weighs Minnesota's Bid to Ban Prediction Markets
item.description: A federal judge is considering whether Minnesota can enforce its ban on prediction markets after Kalshi and Polymarket filed suit to block the state law.

item.title: Kalshi Suspicious Bets Trigger Spotify Stream Audit on Hit Song
item.description: Spotify stripped millions of streams from a chart-topping track after unusual betting patterns on Kalshi's prediction markets raised red flags about possible manipulation.

item.title: Kalshi Trader's Fraud Tip Forces Spotify to Confirm Stream Manipulation
item.description: A Kalshi prediction-market participant flagged anomalous streaming data that prompted Spotify to publicly acknowledge a fraud scheme affecting its platform.

item.title: Supreme Court Gets First Filing in Sports Prediction Market Battle
item.description: A legal dispute over sports-event prediction markets has reached the U.S. Supreme Court, marking the first filing at that level in the fight over whether such contracts are legal.

item.title: New Jersey Takes Kalshi Sports Contract Fight to Supreme Court
item.description: New Jersey is preparing a Supreme Court petition challenging Kalshi's right to offer sports-event contracts, escalating a legal battle over federal versus state gambling authority.

item.title: Polymarket Whale Drops $400K on Putin Losing Power by 2026
item.description: An anonymous Polymarket user has placed a $400,000 wager predicting Russian President Vladimir Putin will lose power before the end of 2026.

item.title: Thin Markets and Bots Plague Most Prediction Contract Listings
item.description: The vast majority of prediction market contracts trade at low volumes, exposing users to price manipulation, erratic swings, and automated trading exploitation, according to CNBC.

item.title: Cboe Seeks SEC Blessing for Earnings-Based Event Contracts
item.description: Cboe has formally asked the SEC to approve a new class of event contracts tied to corporate earnings outcomes, expanding the regulated event-contract landscape beyond the CFTC's domain.

item.title: Large Polymarket Wagers Placed on Putin Losing Power
item.description: Significant sums have been wagered on Polymarket contracts tied to Russian President Vladimir Putin's political fate, drawing widespread attention.

item.title: Kalshi Sues Illinois Over Sports Betting Tax on Prediction Markets
item.description: Kalshi is challenging Illinois's application of its sports betting tax to prediction market contracts, escalating a nationwide fight over how event contracts should be regulated.

item.title: Prediction Markets Reach $24B Monthly Volume as States Push Back
item.description: Monthly trading volume on prediction markets has surged to $24 billion, triggering a wave of state-level regulatory resistance across the U.S.
